title: Admin First-Time Registration, Login, and Dashboard Management Flow

requirements.text: Implement a first-time admin registration flow: when no admin account exists in the backend, redirect any visit to /admin or /admin/login to /admin/register. The registration page must collect email and password, hash the password with SHA-256, and call the backend's seed/invite admin mutation to create the first admin account. After successful registration, redirect to /admin/login.

quotes: Need to add admin login rigister first time

acceptanceCriteria: When no admin exists, navigating to /admin or /admin/login redirects to /admin/register
The registration form validates that email and password fields are non-empty and that password meets minimum length
On successful submission, the first admin account is created in the backend and the user is redirected to /admin/login
After an admin account exists, /admin/register redirects to /admin/login and is no longer accessible directly

requirements.text: Implement the admin login page at /admin/login that supports email/password authentication. On successful credential verification (using SHA-256 password hashing and the backend verifyAuthentication method), persist the admin session to sessionStorage and redirect to /admin/dashboard. Display a clear error message on failed login.

quotes: after login i can change from admin dashboard

acceptanceCriteria: The login form accepts email and password inputs
On successful login, admin session is saved in sessionStorage and the user is redirected to /admin/dashboard
On failed login, an inline error message is displayed without clearing the email field
The login page is accessible when no admin session exists and redirects to /admin/dashboard if already logged in

requirements.text: Protect all /admin/* routes with AdminGuard so that only authenticated admins (verified via sessionStorage session) can access the admin dashboard and its sub-pages. Unauthenticated users attempting to access any admin route must be redirected to /admin/login. If no admin exists yet, redirect to /admin/register instead.

quotes: after login i can change from admin dashboard

acceptanceCriteria: Visiting any /admin/* page without a valid session redirects to /admin/login
Visiting any /admin/* page when no admin account exists redirects to /admin/register
AdminGuard correctly allows access when a valid admin session is present in sessionStorage
Logging out clears the sessionStorage session and redirects to /admin/login

requirements.text: Ensure the admin dashboard page at /admin/dashboard is fully accessible and functional after login, displaying all management section links (quotations, projects, content, media, users, etc.) and a working logout button that clears the session and redirects to /admin/login.

quotes: after login i can change from admin dashboard

acceptanceCriteria: After successful login, the admin is taken to /admin/dashboard
All management navigation links on the dashboard are clickable and route to their respective admin pages
The logout button clears sessionStorage and redirects to /admin/login
The dashboard shows quotation statistics and management options

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
All Motoko logic must remain in backend/main.mo as a single actor
Password must always be hashed with SHA-256 before being sent to or compared in the backend
nonGoals: Real-time dashboard updates or WebSocket integration
